title: JWT access token stored in localStorage
description: The bearer access token (pw-access-token) and username are persisted to localStorage. Any XSS payload — from a third-party script, a future innerHTML sink, or a compromised dependency — can exfiltrate the token via localStorage.getItem and replay it from outside the origin.
recommendation: Move the access token to an HttpOnly session cookie managed by the backend. If cookie-based auth is not feasible short-term, add token-binding checks server-side and document the residual risk.

title: Axios interceptor reads bearer token from localStorage on every request
description: The shared axiosInstance interceptor reads pw-access-token from localStorage and attaches it as an Authorization header on all API calls. This is the same XSS-exfiltrable surface as TS-001 and means any future logging middleware would capture bearer tokens in plain text.
recommendation: Align with the TS-001 fix: store the token in an in-memory variable and read it from there instead of localStorage.

title: downloadArtifact attaches JWT to a caller-supplied URL without origin validation
description: The function accepts an arbitrary url string and unconditionally attaches the bearer token retrieved from localStorage. If a caller passes a URL resolving to an attacker-controlled host the token is leaked cross-origin. Current callers pass relative paths but the type constraint is string with no enforcement.
recommendation: Validate that url begins with a known same-origin prefix (e.g. /api/v1/) before attaching the Authorization header; throw or strip the header otherwise.

title: Open redirect via under-validated from query parameter after login
description: After login the code computes safeFrom by rejecting paths that start with /namespaces/ but does not validate the value is a same-origin relative path. A crafted URL such as //evil.com passes the check; react-router navigate() normalises the double-slash and may redirect to an external origin in some browsers.
recommendation: Validate that safeFrom starts with a single / and does not start with // before calling navigate(); otherwise fall back to /.

title: Multiple useEffect hooks with missing dependency arrays in CatalogPage
description: Three useEffect calls have incomplete dep arrays: fetchNamespaces() runs with [] (stale closure if function identity changes); the second effect omits setNamespace and fetchNamespaceRole; the third omits fetchPlugins and fetchTags. This silently skips re-runs when stable Zustand action references change.
recommendation: Add all referenced callbacks to each dep array. Enable the exhaustive-deps ESLint rule project-wide to prevent recurrence.

title: Concurrent setFilters + fetchPlugins in useEffect can produce stale-response races
description: The third useEffect calls store.setFilters() synchronously and then awaits fetchPlugins(). If the component unmounts between those two calls or a second effect fires before the response resolves the store ends up in an inconsistent state and the late response overwrites a newer one.
recommendation: Introduce an AbortController in the effect cleanup or derive filter state from URL search params so React Router handles re-fetching on navigation.

title: pluginStore duplicates server-authoritative data without cache invalidation
description: usePluginStore holds plugins, totalElements, totalPages, pendingReviewPluginCount, and availableTags — all server-derived data fetched imperatively with no TTL or invalidation logic. Any background navigation leaves the store showing stale data from a previous namespace without the user being aware.
recommendation: Migrate catalog list fetching to a server-state library (TanStack Query or SWR) to get automatic stale-while-revalidate and per-query cancellation.

title: namespaceStore and authStore maintain overlapping namespace data with no shared invalidation
description: namespaceStore fetches /namespaces and caches the list while authStore tracks the active namespace slug independently. The two stores have no coordinated invalidation; a namespace delete in NamespacesSection manually calls both stores methods creating a brittle coupling.
recommendation: Consolidate namespace fetching into a single query layer or use a server-state library so all consumers subscribe to the same cache entry.

title: NamespaceDetailView fetches full namespace list to find one entry then discards the rest
description: namespacesApi.listNamespaces() is called and the result array is searched for the slug matching the URL param. This wastes bandwidth proportional to namespace count and breaks if the API introduces server-side pagination.
recommendation: Use a dedicated GET /namespaces/{slug} endpoint or pass the namespace data via a route loader from the parent page.

title: Non-null assertion on optional rel.changelog after truthiness filter
description: rel.changelog! is used inside a .map() block even though the preceding .filter(r => r.changelog) guarantees trueness. TypeScript does not narrow the type through a non-predicate filter so the assertion is redundant and will throw if the filter is ever relaxed.
recommendation: Use a type-predicate filter or replace rel.changelog! with rel.changelog ?? .

title: Non-null assertion on plugin.status without preceding null guard in PluginCard
description: plugin.status! is called twice in the status badge render path without a null check. If the API returns a plugin without a status field this throws at runtime and crashes the entire card grid.
recommendation: Use optional chaining: plugin.status?.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + (plugin.status?.slice(1) ?? ) or add an explicit early-return guard.

title: Non-null assertion on plugin.status without preceding null guard in PluginListRow
description: Same pattern as TS-011 in PluginListRow.tsx: plugin.status!.charAt(0) is called without a null guard crashing the list view row if status is absent.
recommendation: Apply the same fix as TS-011.

title: Non-null assertion on namespace in action handlers where null is valid
description: namespace! is used in handleApprove and handleReject. The useEffect guard checks for !namespace before fetching but the action handlers are callable while namespace could transition to null during a namespace switch.
recommendation: Add an explicit early-return guard (if (!namespace) return;) at the start of both handlers before the API call.

title: useUploadFiles useCallback has an empty dep array hiding stale-closure risk
description: uploadFiles is memoised with useCallback([]) but internally reads all state via Zustand getState() escapes. The empty array is correct now but is undocumented making it a maintenance trap: any future reactive value added inside the callback would silently become stale.
recommendation: Add a comment explaining why [] is safe (all reads escape the closure via getState()) so future maintainers understand the invariant.

title: Namespace fetch failure silently swallowed with empty catch block
description: In NamespaceDetailView the catch block for namespacesApi.listNamespaces() is empty (/* ignore */) and setLoaded(true) is always called. A network failure silently shows an empty form with no user-visible error.
recommendation: Catch the error into state and render an Alert or call addToast so the user knows why the fields are empty.

title: Redundant non-null assertion in CopyableSha256.handleCopy after early-return guard
description: value! is used inside handleCopy() even though the early return at line 49 already ensures value is defined. TypeScript can narrow after the guard but the assertion overrides that narrowing.
recommendation: Remove the ! and write navigator.clipboard.writeText(value) — TypeScript narrows correctly after the early-return guard.

title: Non-null assertion on slugParam without a structural guard
description: const slug = slugParam! asserts the URL param is always present. React Router does not guarantee the param exists if the route config changes or the component is rendered outside its matched route.
recommendation: Add a runtime guard (if (!slugParam) return null;) or use const { slug: slugParam =  } = useParams<{ slug: string }>();

title: Logout fire-and-forget fetch swallows errors silently
description: logout() fires fetch to /api/v1/auth/logout with .catch(() => {}) without awaiting. If the server is unreachable the local auth state is cleared immediately while the server session remains active and the failure is invisible to the user.
recommendation: At minimum log the rejection; preferably await the fetch with a short timeout and show a toast on failure before clearing local state.

title: Scalar SDK color mode written to localStorage outside the pw- key namespace
description: setItem(colorMode ...) writes to localStorage using a key that does not follow the pw- naming convention used by all other persisted state adding to the localStorage attack surface and making auditing harder.
recommendation: Remove the setItem call and rely solely on the CSS class toggle already applied to document.body which is sufficient for Scalar theme switching.

title: No React ErrorBoundary wraps route-level page components
description: Neither AppShell nor any route element wraps its children in a React ErrorBoundary. An unhandled render-time exception in any page — e.g. from an unexpected null in an API response — crashes the entire application tree and shows a blank page.
recommendation: Add an ErrorBoundary around the Outlet in AppShell using react-error-boundary or a custom class component with a fallback to Error500Page.

title: Mobile hamburger menu button has no onClick handler
description: The IconButton with aria-label Open menu renders with a Menu icon but has no onClick. On viewports narrower than md all primary navigation links are hidden and the hamburger is the only affordance making the app functionally unusable on mobile.
recommendation: Implement a Drawer or Sheet component for mobile navigation and wire the button onClick to open it.

title: Dead barrel re-exports of admin section components flagged by knip
description: knip.json reports GeneralSection, UsersSection, OidcProvidersSection, and ReviewsSection as unused exports from AdminSettingsPage.tsx. Each is already imported directly from its own module file in router/index.tsx via lazy().
recommendation: Remove the four named re-exports from AdminSettingsPage.tsx.

title: updatesApi exported but never consumed by hand-written code (knip)
description: knip.json flags updatesApi as an unused export. No component or hook imports it suggesting the update-check feature is not yet wired to the UI.
recommendation: Remove the export from config.ts or implement the update-check UI that consumes it.

title: Non-null assertion on Intl.supportedValuesOf before availability check
description: intlWithSupport.supportedValuesOf! is called after a conditional branch that supposedly guarantees support but the ! bypasses TypeScript type narrowing. On older runtimes where supportedValuesOf is undefined this throws a TypeError for all users loading the timezone selector.
recommendation: Replace with an explicit typeof guard: if (typeof intlWithSupport.supportedValuesOf === function) and fall back to a bundled IANA timezone list otherwise.
